name: Ross Gorman
abstract: John Ross Smeed Gorman (November 18, 1890 – February 27, 1953) was an American jazz clarinetist, bandleader, and multi-instrumentalist. Gorman is best remembered for his work with Paul Whiteman, particularly his famous clarinet glissando for Rhapsody in Blue, on which he also played oboe, bass clarinet, and saxophone.

concerts.label: Captain Eugene LaBarre and His World's Fair Band

about_quotes.speaker: Wild Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: Red told Ross Gorman that maybe he ought to go up and hear me play. And, maybe I could take his place. And, so, Ross Gorman said -- do you think you could handle it - and I said, well, with my reading - if you let me take the parts and take them home -- he said, well, that can be arranged---

about_quotes.speaker: Snub Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: that's how I met Michael F?, Ross Gorman and Henry Buzzy, all those guys... Paul Whiteman had his whole band. He had already arranged for them to set up a table for us.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: I remember hearing a saxophone player at the time, Ross Gorman, and he had an orchestra and he played at a club, I believe it was called the Monte Carlo Club.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: I got to hear Whiteman later on... Ross Gorman, he played it and I still think he plays it -- he understands it.

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: I knew all of them, you know. I knew all the saxaphone players, I knew Ross Gorman, big names like that
